title: 10 best herbal capsules for over bleeding during periods
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for over bleeding during periods are a natural supplement designed to alleviate excessive menstrual bleeding, also known as menorrhagia. These capsules typically contain a blend of herbal extracts such as turmeric, ginger, and Vitex agnus-castus, which help to reduce blood loss by soothing the uterus and promoting healthy circulation. For example, Turméricare and Gingerplex capsules have been shown to significantly reduce menstrual flow and cramping, allowing women to return to their daily activities with ease. By taking these herbal supplements, women can experience improved quality of life during their periods, reducing anxiety and stress related to heavy bleeding.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai capsules helps with over bleeding during periods because they contain a blend of herbs that work synergistically to regulate hormonal imbalances. The Chinese angelica root in dong quai has been traditionally used to treat menstrual disorders, including excessive bleeding. By normalizing blood flow and promoting uterine contractions, dong quai capsules help reduce heavy bleeding and alleviate cramps associated with menstruation. Additionally, the capsules' anti-inflammatory properties may also contribute to a reduction in bleeding, making them a natural and effective remedy for managing period-related issues.
remedy_properties: Antihemorrhagic properties: Dong Quai capsules contain compounds that help to stop or reduce bleeding by promoting blood clotting and platelet aggregation, which can alleviate heavy menstrual flow.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The capsules' anti-inflammatory effects may help to reduce inflammation in the uterus and cervix, which can contribute to excessive bleeding during menstruation.

Blood vessel-constricting properties: Dong Quai's ability to constrict blood vessels may help to reduce blood flow to the uterus, which can decrease menstrual bleeding and alleviate symptoms of heavy periods.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, particularly ferulic acid and sinapic acid, have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce menstrual cramps and alleviate bleeding by regulating the uterine contractions.

Ligustilide: Ligustilide, a sesquiterpene lactone, has a relaxing effect on the smooth muscles of the uterus, thereby reducing uterine contractions and helping to control excessive bleeding during periods.

Butylphthalide: Butylphthalide, a sesquiterpene lactone, has a vasodilatory effect, which helps to improve blood circulation and reduce menstrual blood loss by dilating the blood vessels and promoting the coagulation of blood.
remedy_parts: Roots: These are the primary source of the medicinal compounds responsible for regulating menstrual flow and reducing heavy bleeding.
Barks: The barks contain a significant amount of ferulic acid and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce menstrual bleeding and alleviate symptoms of menorrhagia.

Leaves: The leaves of Angelica sinensis are also used to make capsules, as they contain flavonoids and other compounds that have a regulating effect on the menstrual cycle and help to reduce excessive bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30g of dried angelica sinensis root from a trusted supplier or cultivated field.
Grind the dried angelica sinensis root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 5g of the powder with 5g of microcrystalline cellulose in a small bowl.
Use a capsule filling machine to fill 100-200 capsules with the powder mixture.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dry place within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree capsules helps with over bleeding during periods because they contain a potent combination of flavonoids, terpenes, and other bioactive compounds that work in harmony to alleviate menstrual cramps and heavy flow. The capsules' anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce uterine contractions, while their antioxidant properties protect the endometrium from damage, leading to a decrease in bleeding intensity and duration. Additionally, the capsules may also help to regulate hormonal balance, further contributing to reduced bleeding and a more manageable menstrual experience.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties: Ginkgo biloba extracts have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax uterine muscles, which can help alleviate heavy bleeding and cramping associated with menstrual periods.

Antioxidant and free radical scavenging activities: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties can help neutralize excessive oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to excessive bleeding during menstruation.

Vasodilatory effects: The extracts of Ginkgo biloba have been found to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, which can help reduce menstrual bleeding and alleviate symptoms such as heavy periods, cramps, and bloating.
remedy_constituents: Bilobalide: This terpene helps with over bleeding during periods by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may reduce menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding.

Ginkgolides: These terpenoids help with over bleeding during periods by inhibiting platelet-activating factor (PAF) and thrombin, which may reduce blood clotting and alleviate heavy menstrual bleeding.

Flavonoids (including Quercetin, Kaempferol, and Isorhapontigenin): These phenolic compounds help with over bleeding during periods by exerting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may reduce menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding, as well as regulate hormones and improve blood vessel function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to regulate menstrual bleeding.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds are known to have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce heavy menstrual bleeding and alleviate cramps.
(Optional) Buds: Some herbal remedies use Ginkgo buds, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that may help to reduce bleeding and alleviate symptoms of menstrual disorders.
remedy_recipe: Sift 200 grams of dried ginkgo biloba leaves through a fine-mesh sieve to remove lumps and debris.
Weigh out 150 grams of gelatin capsule shells and soak them in 200 milliliters of cold water.
Combine 20 milliliters of glycerin and 20 milliliters of distilled water in a small saucepan to heat.
Add the sifted ginkgo biloba leaves to the heated glycerin mixture and stir for 15 minutes to infuse.
Use a capsule filler to fill the gelatin capsule shells with the infused ginkgo biloba mixture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony capsules helps with over bleeding during periods because they contain a unique blend of herbs that help to regulate menstrual flow. The peony root, in particular, is known for its ability to stop excessive bleeding by tonifying the blood and stopping internal bleeding. Additionally, the capsules also help to calm the uterus and alleviate cramps, further reducing the amount of bleeding. By addressing the root causes of heavy periods, Chinese peony capsules provide natural relief and promote overall menstrual health.
remedy_properties: Antihemorrhagic property: The capsules contain compounds that help to stop or reduce bleeding by constricting blood vessels and reducing blood flow to the uterus, thereby alleviating heavy menstrual bleeding.

Stabilizing effect on blood: Chinese Peony Capsules have been shown to stabilize blood clots and prevent their formation, which can help to reduce excessive bleeding and alleviate symptoms of menorrhagia.

Regulating effect on uterine contractions: The capsules contain compounds that help to regulate uterine contractions, which can help to reduce heavy menstrual bleeding by reducing the frequency and intensity of contractions.
remedy_constituents: Isoquinoline alkaloids: These compounds, particularly paeoniflorin and paeonol, help to reduce bleeding by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause the uterus to contract and lead to heavy bleeding.

Flavonoids: Paeonia lactiflora contains flavonoids such as quercetin and kaempferol, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, helping to reduce bleeding by soothing the uterine muscles and preventing excessive contractions.

Triterpenoid saponins: These compounds, including paeoniflorin and paeonol, have been shown to have a direct effect on the uterus, helping to reduce bleeding by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins and other hormone-like substances that cause heavy bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain compounds that help to stabilize and tone the uterus, reducing heavy menstrual bleeding.
Rhyzomes: They have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the uterus, reducing inflammation and heavy bleeding.
Buds: They are rich in flavonoids and other compounds that can help to reduce inflammation and improve blood circulation, which can help to alleviate heavy menstrual bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest paeonia lactiflora roots in the autumn season after the plant has reached maturity.
Dry the harvested roots at a temperature not exceeding 40 degrees celsius for 2 days.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered root with a suitable capsule filler material in a 1:1 ratio.
Fill the mixture into gelatin capsules using an automated capsule filling machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn capsules helps with over bleeding during periods because of its natural ability to tone and strengthen the uterine muscles, reducing excessive blood flow. The herb's astringent properties also help to constrict blood vessels, which can alleviate heavy menstrual bleeding. Additionally, hawthorn has anti-inflammatory properties that may reduce cramping and discomfort associated with over bleeding. By promoting healthy circulation and regulating blood flow, hawthorn capsules can provide natural relief for women experiencing excessive bleeding during their menstrual cycle.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Hawthorn's antioxidant compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to excessive menstrual bleeding by reducing uterine contractions and improving blood vessel walls.

Vasorelaxing properties: Hawthorn's flavonoids and procyanidins can relax the smooth muscles in blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and alleviating vasoconstriction, which can help reduce heavy bleeding during menstruation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Hawthorn's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce prostaglandin production, which is responsible for causing uterine contractions and increased menstrual bleeding.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Crataegus monogyna, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have been found to have anti-inflammatory and anticoagulant properties, which may help reduce heavy menstrual bleeding by inhibiting platelet aggregation and improving blood vessel function.

Triterpenes: Triterpenes, such as oleanolic acid and ursolic acid, in Crataegus monogyna may help alleviate heavy menstrual bleeding by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can reduce prostaglandin production and alleviate uterine cramping.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, including chlorogenic acid and caffeic acid, in Crataegus monogyna have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which may help reduce heavy menstrual bleeding by relaxing uterine smooth muscles and reducing prostaglandin production.
remedy_parts: Fruits: They are used due to their astringent properties, which help to contract the blood vessels and reduce excessive bleeding.
Leaves: They are used for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to stop excessive bleeding and reduce menstrual cramps.
Buds: They are used due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to stop excessive bleeding and reduce menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the required amount of dried crataegus monogyna leaves and flowers about 10 grams.
Grind the dried material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix the ground powder with a binding agent like gelatin in a ratio of 1:1 for 5 minutes.
Fill a size 00 capsule with the mixture and seal it immediately using a capsule filler.
Label and store the capsules in a cool dry place away from direct sunlight.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Silk tree capsules helps with over bleeding during periods because of its natural ability to regulate menstrual flow. The capsules contain a compound called albizzin, which has been shown to constrict blood vessels and reduce excessive bleeding. Additionally, the herb has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the uterus and alleviate cramps associated with heavy bleeding. By taking silk tree capsules regularly, women can experience relief from prolonged or heavy menstrual bleeding, allowing them to manage their periods more effectively.
remedy_properties: Astringent Properties: Herbal Silk Tree Capsules have astringent properties, which help to constrict or reduce blood vessel size, thereby decreasing menstrual flow and alleviating heavy bleeding.

Antioxidant Activity: The capsules possess antioxidant properties that help to neutralize free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the uterus, which can contribute to heavy menstrual bleeding.

Haemostatic Effect: Herbal Silk Tree Capsules have a haemostatic effect, meaning they help to promote blood clotting and reduce bleeding by inhibiting platelet aggregation and promoting the production of thromboxane A2.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Albizia julibrissin contains flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds may help reduce menstrual bleeding by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which can cause uterine contractions and bleeding.

Isoflavones: Albizia julibrissin is a rich source of isoflavones, particularly genistein and daidzein. Isoflavones may help regulate menstrual bleeding by influencing estrogen receptors and modulating the production of prostaglandins, thus reducing the severity of menstrual bleeding.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid, may also be present in Albizia julibrissin. These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which could help reduce menstrual bleeding by protecting the uterine lining from oxidative stress and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties.
Buds: They contain compounds that help to reduce bleeding and alleviate menstrual discomfort.
Flowers: They are used to treat excessive menstrual bleeding due to their emollient and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of dried albizia julibrissin flowers in a clean and dry environment to ensure optimal quality.
Mix the dried flowers with 500g of powdered vegetable cellulose in a large bowl to achieve a uniform blend.
Use a high-speed mixer to blend the mixture for 5 minutes at a speed of 3000 rpm.
Fill 2000 empty gelatin capsules with the blended mixture using a capsule filler machine at a rate of 100 capsules per minute.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool and dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with over bleeding during periods because they contain natural compounds that help to regulate hormone levels, particularly estrogen. The capsules are rich in phytoestrogens, which have been shown to mimic the effects of estrogen and help to reduce heavy menstrual bleeding. Additionally, dandelion root has anti-inflammatory properties that may also contribute to its ability to alleviate bleeding symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting healthy blood clotting. By addressing hormonal imbalances and inflammation, dandelion capsules can provide effective relief from excessive bleeding during menstruation.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Dandelion capsules contain tannins, which have astringent properties that help to constrict blood vessels and reduce blood flow, thereby reducing excessive menstrual bleeding.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds like kaempferol, which can help to reduce inflammation in the uterus and surrounding tissues, which may contribute to heavy bleeding during menstruation.

Hormone-regulating properties: Dandelion contains phytoestrogens, which may help to regulate hormonal imbalances that can lead to excessive menstrual bleeding by normalizing estrogen levels and promoting healthy uterine function.
remedy_constituents: Taraxasterol: This triterpenoid saponin in Taraxacum officinale capsules has anti-inflammatory and anticoagulant properties, which may help to reduce menstrual bleeding and alleviate symptoms of over bleeding.

Taraxasterol acetate: This triterpenoid saponin also found in Taraxacum officinale capsules, may help to reduce menstrual bleeding by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, which are hormones that stimulate uterine contractions and bleeding.

Taraxasterol and its derivatives ( Taraxasterol esters), have a significant influence on the anti-inflammatory response, reducing bleeding. However, Phenolic acids (e.g., Caffeic acid, Ferulic acid): These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce menstrual bleeding and alleviate symptoms of over bleeding by regulating hormone levels, reducing oxidative stress, and inhibiting the production of inflammatory mediators.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are used due to their high concentration of tannins, which have astringent properties that help to reduce bleeding.
Roots: The roots are used because they contain a high amount of inulin, a prebiotic that helps to regulate hormones and reduce heavy menstrual bleeding.
Flowers: The flowers are used due to their high content of flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that help to reduce menstrual cramps and bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in the morning after the dew has dried for optimal potency.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material by spreading it on a paper bag for 24 hours.
Grind the dried taraxacum officinale into a fine powder using a coffee grinder at 1:4 root to leaf ratio.
Mix 1 gram of the ground plant powder with 1 gram of vegetable cellulose in a bowl.
Fill a capsule machine with the plant powder mixture and compress at 1000 pounds force for 5 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree capsules helps with over bleeding during periods because they contain a natural extract that regulates hormonal balance, particularly progesterone levels. This helps to normalize uterine lining shedding, reducing excessive blood flow and alleviating symptoms of heavy menstrual bleeding. Additionally, the herb's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to reduce swelling and ease cramps associated with dysmenorrhea, making it an effective natural remedy for addressing menstrual irregularities.
remedy_properties: Daucosterol content: Chaste Tree capsules contain daucosterol, a compound that helps to regulate estrogen and progesterone levels in the body, which can help to reduce excessive bleeding during menstruation by normalizing hormonal imbalances.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in Chaste Tree capsules help to protect the uterine lining from damage caused by free radicals, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy healing of the uterus during menstruation, which can help to minimize bleeding.

Prostaglandin regulation: Chaste Tree capsules have been shown to regulate prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that play a crucial role in menstruation. By regulating prostaglandins, Chaste Tree capsules may help to reduce excessive bleeding and alleviate other symptoms associated with heavy menstrual flow.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds help regulate hormonal balance and reduce menstrual bleeding by inhibiting the release of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause uterine contractions.

Flavonoids: These polyphenolic compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help stabilize the uterine lining, reducing menstrual bleeding and cramps associated with excessive flow.
Diterpenoid Agnuside: This specific iridoid glycoside has been shown to have a direct effect on the uterine smooth muscle, reducing its contractility and subsequent menstrual bleeding.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have been shown to have a regulating effect on the menstrual cycle.
Seeds: Used due to their rich content of ursolic acid, which has been found to have a positive effect on menstrual irregularities and heavy bleeding.
Leaves: Used due to their high content of flavonoids and other compounds that may help to stabilize and regulate the menstrual cycle.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of dried vitex agnus castus flowers and 500 grams of gelatin powder.
Weigh 250 grams of dried flowers and mix with 500 grams of gelatin powder for 10 minutes.
Combine the gelatin-flower mixture with 1000 milliliters of distilled water in a saucepan and heat to 80 degrees celsius.
Simmer the mixture for 3 hours then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a glass bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the strained liquid and seal immediately to preserve the herbal extract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with over bleeding during periods because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. This compound helps to reduce inflammation in the uterus, which can contribute to excessive bleeding during menstruation. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has been found to help tone the uterine lining, reducing its tendency to bleed excessively. By taking herbal licorice capsules regularly, women may experience a reduction in heavy menstrual bleeding and cramping, providing relief from uncomfortable symptoms associated with PMS.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a key compound in licorice root, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the uterine lining, which can lead to heavy menstrual bleeding.
Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice root have been shown to exert a vasoconstrictive effect, helping to narrow blood vessels and reduce bleeding by constricting the uterine arteries.

Saponins: Saponins in licorice root have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to soothe and calm the uterus, reducing inflammation and cramping associated with heavy menstrual bleeding.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules may help to reduce menstrual bleeding by inhibiting the activity of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that stimulate uterine contractions and bleeding.

Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce menstrual bleeding by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins and other inflammatory mediators.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules, such as isoflavones and flavanones, may help to reduce menstrual bleeding by modulating the activity of estrogen receptors, which can reduce the proliferation of uterine cells and subsequent bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help in reducing menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding.
Barks: They are also rich in glycyrrhizin and other compounds that help in reducing inflammation and promoting healing, which can help alleviate symptoms of heavy menstrual bleeding.

Leaves: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce menstrual cramps, heavy bleeding, and other symptoms associated with PMS.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root and sift it through a fine-mesh sieve.
Grind the sifted root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix 1 gram of the powdered root with 1 gram of gelatin and 1 tablespoon of purified water.
Place the mixture into empty gelatin capsules and seal them tightly with a capsule sealer.
Label and store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle capsules helps with over bleeding during periods because it has a natural ability to constrict blood vessels, reducing excessive menstrual flow. The herb's flavonoids and saponins work together to strengthen uterine walls and stabilize hormone levels, leading to a more balanced menstrual cycle. By regulating uterine contractions and blood vessel tone, stinging nettle capsules help alleviate heavy bleeding, cramping, and discomfort associated with menstruation.
remedy_properties: Vitamin K: Stinging nettle contains high amounts of vitamin K, which helps to promote blood clotting and reduce excessive bleeding during menstruation.
Iron: Nettle is rich in iron, an essential mineral that plays a crucial role in the production of hemoglobin, which helps to carry oxygen throughout the body and reduce menstrual bleeding.

Astringent properties: The astringent compounds present in nettle capsules help to constrict blood vessels and reduce capillary fragility, thereby decreasing excessive menstrual bleeding and cramping.
remedy_constituents: Astringent Tannins: These compounds help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding by promoting blood clotting and preventing excessive blood loss.

Alkaloids (Primarily Caffeic and Chlorogenic Acids): These alkaloids have been shown to have a vasoconstrictive effect, which may help reduce menstrual flow and alleviate heavy bleeding by narrowing the blood vessels and reducing blood flow to the uterus.

Fibers and Saponins (Specifically, Urticulin): These compounds may help to reduce menstrual bleeding by promoting the formation of blood clots and preventing excessive blood loss through their astringent and hemostatic properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain high levels of iron, which helps to alleviate anemia and heavy menstrual bleeding.
Roots: The roots are rich in antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and other nutrients that help to regulate menstrual cycles and reduce bleeding.
Stems: The stems of Urtica dioica contain high amounts of triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to reduce menstrual bleeding and alleviate symptoms of heavy periods.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-50 grams of dried leaves and stems of urtica dioica from a trusted source.
Dry the harvested material in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2 hours.
Grind the dried material into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 1-2 teaspoons of the powdered urtica dioica with 2-3 teaspoons of capsule powder.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them with a capsule sealer.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with over bleeding during periods because they contain mucilage, a thick, protective gel-like substance that soothes and calms the lining of the uterine walls. This natural compound reduces inflammation and irritation, allowing the menstrual flow to normalize and reducing excessive bleeding. Additionally, marshmallow capsules have anti-inflammatory properties that help to constrict blood vessels, further minimizing bleeding. By promoting a healthy and balanced uterine environment, marshmallow capsules provide effective relief from heavy or prolonged menstrual bleeding.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow root contains mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that can help soothe and protect the uterine lining, reducing inflammation and bleeding.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The plant contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and saponins, which can help reduce prostaglandin production, a hormone that causes contractions and increased blood flow during menstruation, leading to less bleeding.
Astringent properties: Marshmallow root also has astringent properties, which can help constrict the uterine lining and reduce blood flow, further alleviating excessive bleeding.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Mucilages help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and promoting healing, which can help to control excessive bleeding.

Althaea polysaccharides (specifically, rhamnose): Althaea polysaccharides have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help to reduce bleeding and alleviate symptoms of heavy menstrual bleeding.

Althaea flavonoids (such as kaempferol and quercetin): Althaea flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce bleeding, alleviate symptoms of heavy menstrual bleeding, and promote overall uterine health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Althaea officinalis roots are rich in mucilages, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to reduce menstrual bleeding and cramps.

Leaves: Althaea officinalis leaves contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to have estrogenic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to regulate menstrual cycles and reduce bleeding.

Barks: Althaea officinalis barks have been traditionally used to treat menstrual disorders due to their high content of mucilages, which can help to soothe and protect the uterine lining, reducing the risk of over bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the roots of althaea officinalis in late summer or early fall when they are at their peak potency for 4 to 6 weeks.
Dry the harvested roots in a low-temperature oven at 40 degrees celsius for 2 to 3 hours to preserve their medicinal properties.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or a coffee grinder for 30 minutes to 1 hour.
Mix the powdered root with a natural binder such as gelatin or carrageenan in a 1:1 ratio and add a small amount of water to create a uniform paste for 10 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the prepared paste using a capsule filling machine or a manual filling tool for 2 to 3 capsules at a time.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with over bleeding during periods because they contain apigenin, an antioxidant that soothes and calms the uterine lining. This natural relaxant reduces inflammation and constriction of blood vessels, which in turn minimizes excessive menstrual flow. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce cramps and discomfort associated with heavy bleeding. By promoting a balance of hormones and relaxing the uterine muscles, chamomile capsules provide relief from prolonged or heavy periods, allowing women to feel more comfortable and in control during their monthly cycle.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce inflammation in the uterus and surrounding tissues, which can alleviate excessive bleeding during menstruation.

Astringent properties: The astringent compounds in chamomile, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help constrict blood vessels and reduce blood flow to the uterine lining, thereby reducing menstrual bleeding.

Antioxidant properties: Chamomile's antioxidant properties help protect the endometrium from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to excessive bleeding during menstruation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to reduce inflammation and promote blood clotting, aiding in the cessation of excessive menstrual bleeding.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and terpenoids that have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to ease cramps and reduce bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers from a trusted source and store them in an airtight container.
Measure 10g of the harvested flowers into a clean glass jar and add 200ml of vodka or brandy at a ratio of 1:5.
Allow the mixture to steep for 14 days, shaking the jar daily, to facilitate extraction of active compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids and retaining the infused liquid.
Fill #00 capsules with 500mg of the strained liquid and seal immediately to preserve potency for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horse chestnut capsules helps with over bleeding during periods because they contain aesculin, a bioflavonoid that helps to strengthen capillary walls, reducing blood vessel fragility. This leads to a decrease in menstrual bleeding and a reduction in the formation of blood clots, resulting in a more normal and manageable period. The flavonoids present in horse chestnut also have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help to reduce pelvic congestion and alleviate symptoms associated with heavy bleeding.
remedy_properties: Aescin: Aescin, a triterpene saponin found in horse chestnut, has been shown to significantly reduce menstrual blood loss and duration of bleeding due to its ability to improve uterine tone and reduce vasodilation.

Tannins: The high concentration of tannins in horse chestnut capsules helps to constrict blood vessels and stop excessive bleeding by reducing capillary permeability and increasing platelet aggregation.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in horse chestnut capsules, such as quercetin and kaempferol, have anti-inflammatory and anticoagulant properties that help to reduce menstrual blood loss and alleviate symptoms of heavy bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the horse chestnut plant are rich in escin, a compound that helps to reduce menstrual bleeding by constricting blood vessels and preventing excessive loss of blood.
Barks: Horse chestnut barks contain flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to alleviate cramps and reduce heavy bleeding during periods.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried bark of aesculus hippocastanum tree in an amount of 200g.
Grind the harvested bark into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground herb with silica gel in a ratio of 4:1 by weight and store it in an airtight container for 24 hours.
Sift the mixture through a #40 mesh sieve to remove any lumps and package it in glass jars for future use.
Fill empty capsules with the prepared herbal powder using an automatic capsule filler machine and seal them for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese rhubarb capsules helps with over bleeding during periods because of its unique properties that help to stop excessive menstrual flow. The capsule's active ingredient, Rheum palmatum, is a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent that can effectively reduce uterine contractions and alleviate menstrual cramps. Additionally, it has astringent properties that help to constrict blood vessels, thereby reducing bleeding. By addressing the root causes of heavy periods, Chinese rhubarb capsules can provide relief from excessive bleeding and associated discomfort, allowing women to manage their menstruation with greater ease.
remedy_properties: Astringent property: Chinese Rhubarb's astringent property helps to contract and constrict blood vessels, reducing excessive menstrual flow and promoting clotting.
Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory properties of Chinese Rhubarb capsules reduce swelling and inflammation in the pelvic area, which can contribute to heavy menstrual bleeding.

Blood-stopping property: Chinese Rhubarb's ability to stop bleeding is due to its high concentration of tannins, which help to constrict blood vessels and promote clotting, thus reducing menstrual flow.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain anthraquinone glycosides, which help to constrict blood vessels and reduce menstrual flow.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes have been used in traditional medicine to stop excessive uterine bleeding by slowing down the blood circulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality rheum palmatum roots in autumn or spring weighing approximately 500 grams.
Clean and dry the harvested roots in shade for at least 7 days to remove dirt and moisture.
Crush the dried roots into fine powder using a mortar and pestle or food processor.
Mix one part of the powdered root with two parts of tapioca starch and capsule filler to create a uniform blend weighing 250 grams.
Fill empty capsules with approximately 500 milligrams of the herbal blend and store in an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chickweed capsules helps with over bleeding during periods because they contain a unique blend of natural herbs that work together to reduce menstrual cramps, inflammation, and uterine contractions. The anti-inflammatory properties in chickweed help to decrease the severity of menstrual cramps, while its antispasmodic effects relax the uterine muscles, reducing heavy bleeding. Additionally, chickweed's astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels, further reducing excessive bleeding. As a result, women who take chickweed capsules during their periods often experience reduced bleeding and relief from discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chickweed capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and triterpenoid saponins that reduce inflammation in the uterus and surrounding tissues, which can help alleviate heavy bleeding and cramping.

Astringent properties: The plant's high concentration of tannins acts as an astringent, helping to constrict blood vessels and reduce excessive bleeding by drying out and toning the mucous membranes and uterine lining.

Hormone-balancing effects: Chickweed contains phytoestrogens that can help regulate hormonal imbalances in women, which may contribute to heavy menstrual bleeding. By balancing estrogen levels, chickweed capsules can help reduce excessive bleeding and cramping.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chickweed roots have been traditionally used to treat menstrual disorders, including heavy bleeding, due to their ability to help regulate uterine function and reduce inflammation.
Barks: The barks of the chickweed plant contain bioactive compounds that can help constrict blood vessels, reducing excessive bleeding during menstruation.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried stellaria media flowers one ounce per four hundred tablets.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for five minutes.
Mix the ground stellaria media flowers with 20% of silica gel in a bowl for ten minutes.
Fill empty #0 capsules with approximately one-half teaspoon of the stellaria media mixture per capsule for fifteen minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to two years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: European asarabaca capsules helps with over bleeding during periods because they contain a natural extract that has been traditionally used to regulate menstruation. remedy_properties: Aspirin-like compounds: The capsules contain salicylic acid and other aspirin-like compounds, which have anti-inflammatory and anticoagulant effects, helping to reduce blood clotting and ease menstrual cramps.

Flavonoids and phenolic acids: The plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids have antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation, improve blood vessel elasticity, and regulate bleeding patterns.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh asarum europaeum roots and clean them thoroughly with filtered water.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm dark place for 7-10 days or until they become brittle.
Grind dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder.
Mix 200mg of ground asarum europaeum root powder with 100mg of gelatin capsule shells according to manufacturer's instructions.
Fill empty capsules with the mixture and store them in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian capsules helps with over bleeding during periods because it has a calming effect on the uterine muscles, reducing contractions that can lead to heavy bleeding. remedy_recipe: Harvest dried valerian root and measure out 100 grams for the batch.
Grind the dried valerian root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the valerian root powder with 500 milliliters of boiling water to create an herbal decoction.
Steep the mixture for 10 minutes, then strain it through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean container.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with one-half teaspoon of the cooled decoction and store them in an airtight container.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried galega officinalis leaves and flowers in quantities of 250 grams.
Grind the harvested herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Mix 100 grams of the powdered galega officinalis with 50 grams of arrowroot powder in a bowl.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the herbal blend and seal them tightly immediately.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.
